comments.body: Attached 001 patch for changing scheduler configurations via CLI.

Hi [~leftnoteasy]/[~xgong], we discussed directly making REST calls to the sched-conf endpoint from the CLI, but I am wondering if this is the best approach? e.g. the node labels CLI in RMAdminCLI uses the protobuf approach (as in the 001 patch attached here). Going with the CLI as a REST wrapper means we have to manually construct the HTTP request body, which seems possible but not clean.

[~jhung]
In TestRMWebServicesConfigurationMutation#testUpdateQueue(), we have already use REST call to do the queue configuration update.
{code}
    response =
        r.path("ws").path("v1").path("cluster")
            .path("sched-conf").queryParam("user.name", userName)
            .accept(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)
            .entity(toJson(updateInfo, SchedConfUpdateInfo.class),
                MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)
            .put(ClientResponse.class);
{code}

In RMAadminCLI, we only need to parse/combine related arguments into SchedConfUpdateInfo object. 

Protocolbuf approach is OK.  But it is more complicate..
Ah, I forgot about the toJson method...thanks. Will make these changes then.
Uploaded 002 patch containing SchedConfCLI as a wrapper around /sched-conf REST endpoint.

comments.body: Attached 001 patch for changing scheduler configurations via CLI.

Hi [~leftnoteasy]/[~xgong], we discussed directly making REST calls to the sched-conf endpoint from the CLI, but I am wondering if this is the best approach? e.g. the node labels CLI in RMAdminCLI uses the protobuf approach (as in the 001 patch attached here). Going with the CLI as a REST wrapper means we have to manually construct the HTTP request body, which seems possible but not clean.

[~jhung]
In TestRMWebServicesConfigurationMutation#testUpdateQueue(), we have already use REST call to do the queue configuration update.
{code}
    response =
        r.path("ws").path("v1").path("cluster")
            .path("sched-conf").queryParam("user.name", userName)
            .accept(MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)
            .entity(toJson(updateInfo, SchedConfUpdateInfo.class),
                MediaType.APPLICATION_JSON)
            .put(ClientResponse.class);
{code}

In RMAadminCLI, we only need to parse/combine related arguments into SchedConfUpdateInfo object. 

Protocolbuf approach is OK.  But it is more complicate..
Ah, I forgot about the toJson method...thanks. Will make these changes then.
Uploaded 002 patch containing SchedConfCLI as a wrapper around /sched-conf REST endpoint.
